problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A compass is designed to point north, not in the direction of the wind.
Constantly changing one's direction based on external influences prevents the discovery of one's true passion and purpose.
It is important to have a single, unwavering goal in life.
Following popular trends is an unreliable strategy for career planning.
One must look inward to find true direction.
problems.passage: Tony was a bright but indecisive college student, constantly changing his major based on the latest job market trends and his parents' suggestions. One semester he was pre-med, the next he was computer science, and then business. He felt perpetually lost, his future a blurry landscape. His academic advisor, reviewing his chaotic transcript, drew a compass rose on a piece of paper. "A compass that points to every wind that blows," she said, "will never find its own true north." Tony realized he had been looking for direction everywhere but within himself. He needed to discover his own passions and strengths to set a steady course.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Planning is a more efficient use of time than fixing mistakes.
Investing time in careful preparation and planning upfront can prevent much larger problems and save more time later.
It is a common mistake to underestimate the importance of planning.
A lack of planning is the primary cause of failure in construction projects.
One should always consult an expert before starting a major project.
problems.passage:
[지문 87] The Builder's Blueprint

Kevin was eager to build a new deck for his house and decided to skip the "boring" step of creating a detailed blueprint. He thought he could just "figure it out" as he went along, saving time. Halfway through the project, he discovered his measurements were off, the support beams were uneven, and he had wasted a significant amount of expensive lumber. His contractor friend, called in to assess the mess, shook his head. "An hour of planning," he said, "can save you ten hours of fixing." Kevin learned the hard way that proper preparation, far from being a waste of time, is the most effective way to prevent costly errors and ensure a quality result.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to build a large building on a weak foundation.
Complex and impressive achievements cannot be built without a solid mastery of fundamental skills.
One should not neglect the basics in pursuit of advanced skills.
A strong foundation is the most important part of any structure.
True mastery is built upon a foundation of disciplined practice.
problems.passage: Oliver, a gifted young guitarist, was impatient to play flashy, complex solos. He considered the practice of basic scales and chords to be tedious and beneath him. He wanted to jump straight to the performance. His music teacher listened to his frustrated and sloppy attempts at a difficult piece. He then stopped him and played a single, perfectly tuned C-major scale. "You cannot build a cathedral," the teacher said, "on a foundation of sand." Oliver realized that the fundamental skills he disdained were the very bedrock upon which all advanced techniques and true musicality were built.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Fish are sensitive to vibrations in the water.
An anxious and impatient mindset can be counterproductive and hinder success.
A state of inner calm and patience is essential for achieving one's goals.
Sometimes, doing less is more effective than doing more.
Wisdom involves understanding when to act and when to be still.
problems.passage: Sam, a young and energetic fisherman, believed that the more he did, the more fish he would catch. He constantly recast his line, changed his bait, and moved his boat around the small cove. His grandfather, sitting silently on the shore, watched his grandson's frantic activity with an amused expression. After an hour of this, Sam had caught nothing, while the old man's bucket was nearly full. "The fish can hear a restless heart through the line," the grandfather finally called out. Sam learned that a state of calm, patient anticipation was often a more powerful tool than ceaseless, anxious action.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The pursuit of perfection is like chasing a ghost.
Striving for an impossible standard of perfection can prevent one from achieving perfectly acceptable and valuable results.
It is better to complete something imperfectly than to never complete it at all.
Perfectionism can be a major barrier to productivity and creativity.
One should learn to be satisfied with "good enough."
problems.passage: Grace was a talented sculptor but a crippling perfectionist. She would work on a piece for weeks, only to destroy it if she found a single, tiny flaw that deviated from her ideal vision. As a result, she had never completed a major work and was sinking into a state of creative despair. Her mentor, visiting her studio, saw the fragments of her destroyed sculptures. He picked up a piece of a "failed" work. "You are chasing a ghost," he told her. "Perfection is the enemy of the good." Grace realized that her relentless pursuit of an unattainable ideal was preventing her from creating anything good at all. She learned to accept imperfection as part of the process.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A lighthouse provides light for all ships without discrimination.
The true value of a duty is in its steadfast, impartial performance, regardless of who benefits or how often it is needed.
The purpose of a lighthouse is to be a universal symbol of hope.
One should perform their duties without questioning their importance.
Even outdated technology can be crucial in an emergency.
problems.passage: Emma, the solitary keeper of a lighthouse on a remote, fog-shrouded island, often felt her work was an anachronism. In an age of GPS and advanced radar, she wondered if her nightly vigil truly mattered. Ships were rare, and the silence was vast. During a sudden, violent squall that disabled the electronics of a small ferry, her light became its only guide to safety. The ferry's captain later radioed his profound gratitude. Emma's supervisor, hearing the story, sent her a short message: "A lighthouse does not choose which ships to save. It just stands and it shines." Emma understood her purpose was not to be needed always, but to be there, unwavering, for when she was needed most.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The hardest stones are the best materials for detailed sculptures.
The greatest challenges and difficulties often yield the most valuable and refined results.
One must work in harmony with their materials, not against them.
The process of creating something beautiful is often a struggle.
Persistence is required to overcome difficult obstacles.
problems.passage: James, a student of sculpture, was on the verge of quitting. He felt he was fighting the stone, and the stone was always winning. His attempts to impose his will on the marble resulted in fractured, lifeless forms. His master sculptor watched his struggle. He then picked up a particularly stubborn piece of granite, a stone known for its hardness. "Do not fight the stone," he advised. "The stone that resists the chisel the most is the one that holds the finest detail." James learned that the challenge presented by the material was not an obstacle to be overcome, but a quality that, when understood and worked with, could lead to the most profound and lasting beauty.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The earth has a memory of all past actions.
Actions have long-term consequences, and neglecting foundational principles for short-term gains will ultimately lead to failure.
Sustainable success requires working in harmony with natural systems, not exploiting them.
One cannot cheat nature without eventually facing the consequences.
It is important to maintain a balance of giving and taking in any system.
problems.passage: Laura, having inherited her family's farm, was eager to modernize. She dismissed her grandfather's traditional, slow methods of crop rotation and soil enrichment, opting for aggressive, chemical-heavy shortcuts to maximize short-term yield. The first year's harvest was decent, but the second was poor, and by the third, the soil was barren. Her elderly neighbor, who had farmed the adjacent land for fifty years, looked at her depleted fields and said with a sad shake of his head, "The earth keeps a perfect ledger of all debts and all deposits." Laura learned that nature has its own system of accounting, and shortcuts often create debts that must eventually be paid.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to measure the full extent of a teacher's influence.
The impact of a teacher extends far beyond the individual student, multiplying and spreading in unforeseen ways through society.
A teacher's work is like a river that flows continuously.
The true results of education are often indirect and invisible to the teacher.
A single person can have a ripple effect that changes many lives.
problems.passage: Michael, a young teacher in a tough inner-city school, felt his passion waning. His students seemed trapped in difficult circumstances, and his lessons felt like a drop of rain in a vast desert. He questioned if he was making any real impact. Years later, he attended a conference and was approached by a keynote speaker, a renowned scientist he didn't recognize. The scientist introduced herself as a former student from his very first class. "A teacher never knows where their influence stops," she said, her voice thick with emotion. "It is a river that flows into other rivers, creating a sea you will never see." Michael understood that his influence was not a single act, but a chain reaction of inspiration.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Music is not a competition with a clear winner or loser.
A skill or passion should be viewed as a lifelong journey of exploration and personal growth, not a competitive race against others.
One should enjoy the process of learning without being obsessed with the final goal.
The path of a musician is a long and continuous one.
Comparing oneself to others can ruin the joy of a personal pursuit.
problems.passage: Sarah, a dedicated violinist, practiced with fierce discipline. Yet, she was haunted by a sense of inadequacy, constantly comparing her progress to that of child prodigies she saw online. She viewed her musical development as a race, and she felt she was losing. Her wise teacher, sensing her distress, put down his own violin. "Music is not a race to a finish line," he told her gently. "It is a landscape to be explored for a lifetime." This simple shift in metaphor changed everything for Sarah. She stopped seeing music as a competition and started seeing it as a vast, beautiful territory to be explored at her own pace, with its own unique discoveries along the way.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Spring always follows winter in the cycle of seasons.
Within every period of hardship or apparent inactivity, there lies the potential for new beginnings and growth.
One should not give up hope during difficult times.
What appears to be an ending can actually be a period of necessary rest.
Nature teaches us that life is a cycle of death and rebirth.
problems.passage: Tom, a novice gardener, was filled with despair as the first frost of winter arrived. His vibrant summer garden turned brown and appeared to die overnight. He felt all his hard work had been for nothing and was ready to clear the plot. His experienced neighbor stopped him as he was about to pull up a dormant rose bush. "Do not mourn what looks like an end," the old gardener advised. "In the heart of every winter, there is a trembling spring." Tom learned that periods of dormancy and apparent lifelessness are often essential phases of rest and preparation for future renewal and growth.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: It is impossible to get water from a well that has no water.
Creativity, like a well, is a resource that can be depleted and requires periods of rest and new experiences to be replenished.
Forcing oneself to be creative when feeling uninspired is an ineffective strategy.
It is important to take breaks to avoid creative burnout.
To produce creative work, one must first gather new ideas and inspiration.
problems.passage: Jennifer, a prolific novelist, had hit a wall. For months, she had been suffering from a severe case of writer's block, and the words simply would not come. She felt barren, her creativity completely depleted. She forced herself to sit at her desk for eight hours a day, staring at a blank screen, which only deepened her anxiety. Her writing mentor, an older, wiser author, advised her to take a break. "You cannot draw water from an empty well," he told her. "A well must be given time for the rain to refill it." Jennifer learned that creative output requires periods of input and rest, and that forcing creativity can be counterproductive.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: The mountain will remain, but the climber's life is fragile.
Disregarding safety and showing disrespect for a challenge in pursuit of speed or ego can lead to catastrophic failure.
Patience and caution are more important than speed in high-stakes endeavors.
The opportunity to climb a mountain is a privilege that should not be taken for granted.
One's ego can be the greatest obstacle to achieving a goal.
problems.passage: Alex, a young and ambitious climber, was determined to reach the summit of a treacherous peak faster than anyone before him. He ignored his guide's advice to rest and acclimatize properly, pushing himself to the brink of exhaustion. The experienced guide, seeing the dangerous signs of altitude sickness in Alex, physically blocked his path. "The mountain is not going anywhere," the guide said sternly. "But you will be, if you do not respect it." The stark warning cut through Alex's ego-driven haste. He realized that his quest for speed was jeopardizing the journey itself, and that patience and respect for the challenge were prerequisites for success, not signs of weakness.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Cooking with one's heart is more important than cooking with one's head.
To achieve true mastery, one must complement technical perfection with genuine passion, intuition, and emotional expression.
The best dishes are made with love, not just skill.
It is important to take risks and be "foolish" to create something truly great.
Logic and emotion must be balanced in any creative pursuit.
problems.passage: Ryan was a technically brilliant chef. His knife skills were flawless, his plating was artistic, and his understanding of food chemistry was profound. Yet, the head chef of the Michelin-starred restaurant where he worked felt his dishes, while perfect, lacked a certain soul. After tasting Ryan's latest, technically impeccable creation, the head chef gave him some unexpected advice. "You cook with your brilliant head," he said. "Now learn to cook with your foolish heart." Ryan began to understand that the highest level of artistry required not just precision and knowledge, but also intuition, passion, and a willingness to be vulnerable.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Young people prefer complexity, while old people prefer simplicity.
The journey to mastery often involves moving from a fascination with complexity to an appreciation for profound simplicity.
The simplest solutions are often the most difficult to find.
True wisdom lies in understanding the basics, not in creating complicated systems.
The purpose of a lock is to be opened by a key.
problems.passage: On his last day before retirement, the master clockmaker, William, was giving his final lesson to his young apprentice. The apprentice, eager and ambitious, was already sketching designs for highly complex, multi-functional timepieces. William, however, drew the young man's attention to a simple, elegant grandfather clock he had made fifty years prior. It had only one function: to tell time perfectly. "We spend our youth trying to build the most complicated locks," the old master mused. "But we spend our old age searching for the simplest key." The apprentice understood that true mastery lay not in complexity for its own sake, but in the profound, elegant simplicity that comes from a deep understanding of fundamental principles.

problems.questionText: Step 1. 핵심 정보 추출 (Core Idea Extraction)
choices: 아이들의 학업 성취에서 자기 규제가 가장 중요한 요소이다.
아이들에게 자기 조절을 위한 활동을 할 기회가 있어야 한다.
아이들의 지능은 학업 성취도에 영향을 미치지 않는다.
problems.passage: Clancy Blair, who suggests that the ability to self-regulate is more important than intelligence in children's academic achievement, said that children need to be provided with self-regulating activities to decrease impulsiveness and instant satisfaction and to promote attention and awareness of their own and other's thoughts and feelings.

problems.questionText: Step 1. 핵심 정보 추출 (Core Idea Extraction)
choices: 요즘 아이들은 집중력이 좋아서 웹 페이지 제작에 능한 편이다.
요즘 아이들의 집중력 지속 시간은 컴퓨터의 사용 시간에 비례한다.
요즘 아이들은 뜨개질 같은 집중력을 요하는 활동을 좋아한다.
problems.passage: Despite what most people believe, many of today's kids have longer attention spans than we think; and they are passionate about creating not just personal web-sites, but also attractive, useful knit products that mark their presence in the world.

problems.questionText: 다음 문장의 핵심 정보를 가장 잘 파악한 것을 고르시오.
choices: 아이들이 충분히 상상할 수 있는 문제에도 우리는 과학적 설명을 제공하려 한다.
아이들의 상상력 자극을 위해 지나치게 많은 과학적 설명을 지양해야 한다.
아이들의 상상력이 충족되지 않을 경우 과학적 설명이 제공될 필요가 있다.
problems.passage: We offer lengthy scientific explanations as answers to children's questions, when a direct experience of something similar or an image that can live and work in their imagination would be much more satisfying to them.

problems.questionText: 다음 문장의 핵심 정보를 가장 잘 파악한 것을 고르시오.
choices: 연구에 따르면 마감시간에 대한 압박이 없는 자율적인 직장에 속한 사람이 심장질환에 걸릴 확률이 더 낮지만, 일의 융통성은 떨어진다고 한다.
연구에 따르면 상사의 압박과 발언권이 없는 직장인이 심장질환에 걸릴 확률이 더 낮다고 한다.
연구에 따르면 상사가 요구한 마감시간이 없고 발언권을 가질 수 있는 직장인이 심장질환에 걸릴 확률이 더 낮다고 한다.
problems.passage: In a study of 7,400 employees in London, those who felt they had to meet deadlines imposed by someone else and had little say in how they did their work had a 50 percent higher risk of developing symptoms of coronary heart disease than those with more job flexibility.

problems.questionText: 다음 문장의 핵심 정보를 가장 잘 추출한 것을 고르시오.
choices: 지도력 부재로 인해 세상이 예측하기 힘들고 통제하기가 어려워지고 있다.
세상이 험난해지면서 명확하고 안정적인 지도자가 절실하게 요구되고 있다.
세상이 예측할 수 없게 변하면서 안정적인 지도자를 찾기가 힘들어졌다.
problems.passage: As the structures of our world and the conditions of certainty have yielded to an avalanche of change, the extent of our longing for stable/definitive leadership has been exceeded only by the impossibility of finding it.

problems.questionText: Student journalists are taught how to be concise (when writing in a limited space).
choices: peaceful
proper
clear and brief
wordy

problems.questionText: When James worked in the office, his job was sedentary. In contrast, when he became a reporter, he was almost away from his desk.
choices: well paid
sitting a lot
moving around a lot
exciting
problems.passage: When James worked in the office, his job was sedentary. In contrast, when he became a reporter, he was almost away from his desk.

problems.questionText: Pablo always showed animosity toward his teachers by throwing spitballs and mouthing off, but his sister Mary was kind and sweet.
choices: kindness
greet in a friendly way
a strong feeling of dislike and anger
weapons
problems.passage: Pablo always showed animosity toward his teachers by throwing spitballs and mouthing off, but his sister Mary was kind and sweet.

problems.questionText: 문맥상 밑줄 친 'agitated'의 의미로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: very nervous
peaceful
angry
confused
problems.passage: When Marcus entered the classroom, he felt calm. However, as soon as he opened the test paper, he began to feel agitated.

problems.questionText: 문맥상 밑줄 친 'versatile'의 의미로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: able to do one thing
able to communicate well
able to do lots of different things
better at one skill than another
problems.passage: Journalists must be versatile. For instance, they must be good at writing, listening to people, speaking, working quickly, and doing research.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 표현을 고르시오.
choices: persecuted
rewarded
right
conformists
problems.passage: It has always been dangerous to teach men new ideas contrary to those which are generally accepted. The first men who taught that the earth is round were ________.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: unconquerable obstacle
necessary evil
impolite interaction
popular appeal
problems.passage: If the will to communicate is strong enough, language will not be a(n) ________. We always have as a standby, communication by sign language, though it cannot really be called conversation.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: no resistance
less resistance
less speed
full speed
problems.passage: Air resists the motion of objects passing through it. Airplanes and automobiles are streamlined so that they can pass through the air with ________.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: hope
truth
future
experience
problems.passage: We have no way to judge the future except by the past. Our vision into the future is dim but we have some light from the lamp of ________.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: clear and precise
like his talk
interesting to read
widely known
problems.passage: The average person expresses himself differently in writing and speaking. With proper practice the difference can be overcome, and one's writing will become more ________.

problems.questionText: Science does not seek to enforce a moral code of behavior in its practitioners, as much as ancient philosophy did.
choices: Like ancient philosophers, practitioners of science should be held accountable for their moral behavior.
Ancient philosophy placed more pressure on its practitioners to adhere to moral rules regarding behavior than science currently does.
How a moral code of behavior is contested in science differs from how it was done in ancient philosophy.
Science is more rigorous about regulating its practitioners through a moral code of behavior than in ancient philosophy.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Acting in a play requires listening to the audience's applause.
When one's life is driven by the need for others' approval, they lose their autonomy and live according to others' expectations.
It is impossible to please everyone in the audience.
Social media encourages people to live inauthentic lives.
True happiness comes from writing and directing one's own life story.
problems.passage: Sandra lived her life through the screen of her phone, her self-worth fluctuating with the number of likes and positive comments on her social media posts. She meticulously crafted an idealized online persona, constantly checking for others' applause. Her therapist, after listening to her anxieties for several weeks, offered a powerful metaphor. "When you live for the applause of an audience," she said, "you will perform their play, not your own." The words struck a deep chord. Sandra realized she had handed over the direction of her life to others and began the difficult process of disconnecting her self-worth from external validation and rediscovering her own authentic script.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Clay must be properly prepared before it can be shaped.
Valuable learning and skill development come more from analyzing and correcting failures than from easy, initial successes.
It is common for beginners to fail many times before they succeed.
The most important lessons in a craft are learned through physical practice.
One should not be discouraged by early failures.
problems.passage: Marcus, a novice potter, was on the brink of despair. Every time he tried to raise the walls of a pot on the wheel, the clay would inevitably wobble and collapse into a shapeless, muddy heap. He was convinced he lacked the fundamental talent for the craft. His instructor, however, picked up the collapsed clay, kneaded it, and centered it back on the wheel. "The clay that collapses," she said calmly, "teaches the hands far more than the clay that stands perfectly on the first try." Marcus began to see his failures not as evidence of his inadequacy, but as crucial, tactile lessons that were refining his touch and understanding.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Trees with shallow roots are more likely to fall in a storm.
Rapid, superficial growth often lacks the strong foundation necessary to withstand challenges.
It is dangerous to pursue quick results at the expense of solid preparation.
Natural processes are superior to artificial interventions.
True strength is developed slowly and steadily over time.
problems.passage:
[지문 85] The Garden's Roots

Rachel, an impatient gardener, was frustrated that her neighbor's tomato plants seemed to be growing much faster and taller than her own. Determined to catch up, she began to over-water her plants and apply excessive amounts of "miracle-growth" fertilizer. Her plants did shoot up quickly, but they looked weak and spindly. Her experienced gardening mentor observed her efforts and offered a word of caution. "Be careful," he warned. "A tree that grows too fast in the sun often has the shallowest roots in the storm." Rachel learned that slow, steady, natural growth creates a stronger, more resilient foundation than artificially accelerated growth.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: A ship must be tested in a storm to prove its seaworthiness.
Avoiding challenges and difficulties during preparation leads to a lack of readiness for real-world situations.
It is important to train in conditions that are similar to the actual competition.
One's true ability is only revealed when faced with adversity.
Overconfidence gained in easy conditions can be dangerous.
problems.passage: Lisa was a talented swimmer, but she had a habit of always choosing the path of least resistance during training. She would pick the calmest lane in the pool and avoid practicing in challenging conditions (like choppy water or against a current). Her coach observed this pattern with growing concern. Before a major open-water competition, he told her, "A ship that has only ever sailed in a calm sea is not prepared for a storm." During the actual race, when the wind picked up and the water became rough, Lisa struggled immensely, realizing too late that avoiding challenges in training had left her unprepared for the reality of competition.

problems.questionText: 밑줄 친 부분이 이 글에서 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
choices: Wearing too many masks can make one lose their identity.
Constantly trying to imitate others or cater to different expectations prevents the development of one's own authentic style and identity.
A writer should have a consistent and recognizable voice.
It is more important to be authentic than to be versatile.
Finding one's true self is a difficult but necessary journey.
problems.passage: Hannah was a talented writer, but she was also a chameleon. She would adopt the terse style of Hemingway for one story, then the ornate prose of Faulkner for another, trying to please different literary tastes. As a result, her body of work felt disjointed, lacking a core personality. Her writing coach, after reading several of her disparate pieces, gave her a direct piece of feedback. "When you try to wear every mask," he said, "you forget the shape of your own face." Hannah understood that in her effort to be versatile, she had neglected the crucial task of developing her own authentic and recognizable voice.

problems.questionText: 재진술 :
choices: If good things rest on the motivation of greed, it follows that generous people can never do good to others.
If it is possible that people do good things out of greed, it means that greed may have a bright side in it.
Individuals eager to commit themselves to the good in life can retain the trait until they reach old age.
Even greed may count as a valuable thing in a struggling world, as many of greedy people are men of charity.
problems.passage: If greed motivates individuals to work for the good things in life, there may be a silver lining in the trait.

problems.questionText: 재진술 :
choices: Compared with the spending habits of females, those of males are more rational in our society.
Men and women in our society spend money in a clearly different way from each other.
Classifying the spending habits by gender is emerging as a distinct trend in our society.
The similar and different ways of spending money are found between men and women in our society.
problems.passage: If we compare the spending habits of females and males in our society, a sharp distinction emerges.

problems.questionText: 재진술 :
choices: The results of the study will be unbiased through the people who fund it do not want them to be.
Some say the research is biased, but really it will only do exactly what people ordered it to do.
Even though people say it has to be fair, the report will be supported by financial backers.
The research claims to be fair, but it will be forced to say good things about the people who paid for it.
problems.passage: Despite its purported neutrality, the study will be compelled to produce results favorable to its sponsors.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: bold
strong
frightened
prudent
problems.passage: Mountain-climbing involves many risks, and the climber must be alert at all times. Reckless climbers soon meet with an accident. The job is really one for a man who is ________.

problems.questionText: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.
choices: community
vigorous outdoor sports
biological perpetuation
fierce warfare
problems.passage: Man is one of the most formidable of all animals and the only one who persistently chooses to attack his own species. Throughout history, he has never, except for short periods of time, dispensed with ________.

problems.questionText: The old man's hair was sparse rather than thick and full like it was when he was young.
choices: grow quickly
large in number or amount
slim
small in number or amount

problems.questionText: After he was caught cheating, the student suffered the ignominy of being expelled from the school.
choices: frequency
disgrace
agreement
attitude

problems.questionText: In 1898 the Spanish ceded, or surrendered possession of, both Guam and Puerto Rico to the United.
choices: maintained
gave up
purchased
battled
